title: Pivoting Quick Reference
when_to_use: When compromised host reaches networks you cannot

steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Set up SSH local port forwarding to access services behind compromised host
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for pivoting quick reference
steps.expected_output: Accessible local port forwarding SSH connections to internal services

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: Create dynamic SSH tunnel (SOCKS proxy) for routing arbitrary traffic through pivot
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: Functional SOCKS proxy enabling access to internal networks

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Use proxychains to route scanning tools through established pivot tunnels
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: Successful network scanning through proxychains via compromised host

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Implement pass-the-hash attacks using pth-winexe for Windows lateral movement
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: Command execution on Windows systems using stolen NTLM hashes

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Use CrackMapExec for SMB enumeration and exploitation across internal networks
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: Enumerated SMB services and compromised additional systems via credentials

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Document every pivot action with timestamps for attack chain documentation
steps.expected_output: Comprehensive log of all pivoting activities and system access timeline
